Agenda Number:  13
Section Name: NEW BUSINESS
File ID: 20-2346
TO:
City Council
FROM:
Drew Corbett, City Manager
PREPARED BY:
Finance Department
MEETING DATE:
January 21, 2020 
SUBJECT:
Street and Flood Control Improvements – Bond Sale
RECOMMENDATION:
Adopt a Resolution to approve the San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority’s (the “Authority”) sale of revenue 
bonds (the “2020 Bonds”) in a maximum $54 million principal amount to provide financing for public improvements to be 
owned and operated by the City of San Mateo (the “City”) and to provide for the refinancing of outstanding bonds, 
approving related documents,  declaring the intention to reimburse expenditures from bond proceeds, and directing 
certain actions in connection with the sale of such bonds.
BACKGROUND:
The proposed financing will provide funds to finance two new projects, street reconstruction improvements Citywide and 
flood control improvements benefiting the North Shoreview neighborhood, and to refinance the City’s outstanding City of 
San Mateo Joint Powers Financing Authority Revenue Bonds (South Bayfront Flood Control Project), Series 2011A (the 
“2011 Bonds”) (collectively, the “Project”). The two new projects will be financed with the “2020A Bonds,” and the 
refinancing of the 2011 Bonds will be financed with the “2020B Bonds.”
Street Reconstruction
In response to the public and City Council priorities to reconstruct or rehabilitate failed streets, the City developed the 
Smooth Streets Program in 2013. The program is a multi-year endeavor to reconstruct a total of 22 miles that were 
identified as failed streets in 2013, with targeted completion by 2024. Public Works staff develops specific projects 
annually based on budgeted funds and prioritizes them based on criteria such as traffic volume and pedestrian corridor 
accessibility. Since 2013, 11 miles of the original 22 miles of failed streets have been reconstructed.
In November 2015, San Mateo voters approved Measure S, which extended through 2048 the City’s ¼ cent transactions 
and use tax that was set to expire in 2018, as an affirmation of support to provide a funding source for various 
infrastructure projects.  Revenues collected under Measure S are accounted for in the General Fund, and may be used for 
any legally permitted General Fund expenditure.  These revenues have been programmed for application to street 
reconstruction and certain flood control projects (the latter are described below).  A portion of the Measure S revenues 
will be applied directly to street reconstruction costs on a pay-as-you-go basis, and, because street CIP expenditures will 
need to be made before the bulk of the Measure S revenue is available, the City will leverage future Measure S revenues 
by selling bonds, and future Measure S revenues will be applied to debt service on these bonds.
Flood Control
The City maintains a system of levee and flood walls (the “Coyote Point Levee”) that protects the northeastern corner of 
the City known as the North Shoreview neighborhood against high tides, storm surges, and wave action from the San 
Francisco Bay and operates a series of pump stations to drain North Shoreview residual interior flooding during rain 
306 of 425

--- page 307 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
events.
In 2015, the Federal Emergency Management Agency (“FEMA”) decertified the Coyote Point Levee. Decertification means 
that this levee no longer meets National Flood Insurance Program minimum standards for reducing the risks associated 
with a major flood event (a flood with a one percent chance of occurring in any given year). As a result, the area behind 
the Coyote Point Levee, which has approximately 2,800 structures, has been designated as a high-risk area, also known as 
a Special Flood Hazard Area. Flood insurance is required for structures with mortgages from federally regulated or insured 
lenders when a levee becomes decertified or is uncertified. Structures without flood insurance in Special Flood Hazard 
Areas are ineligible for any type of federal assistance in the event of flooding. Flood insurance for structures in Special 
Flood Hazard Areas is several times more expensive than for structures not in such areas.
Improvements necessary to recertify the Coyote Point Levee consist of replacing two pump stations with new structures, 
pumps, electrical equipment, and discharge piping to San Francisco Bay, and raising approximately 1,300 feet of the 
Coyote Point Levee 3 to 5 feet.  The total project cost is estimated at $25 million.  
In 2018, North Shoreview residents approved assessment levies to pay for a portion of the project (approximately $2.4 
million).  The City will pay for the balance of the project using the proceeds of lease revenue bonds secured by General 
Fund revenues.  Future Measure S revenues have been programmed to make the payments on bonds sold to finance these 
flood control projects.  The North Shoreview assessment levies will pay a portion of the debt service on the bonds used to 
finance flood control projects, and the remainder will be paid from the General Fund.
Refinancing of 2011 Bonds
In 2011, the City sold lease revenue bonds to finance improvements to a levee protecting the South Bayfront 
neighborhood.  Those improvements are paid for with assessment levies approved by South Bayfront property owners.  
Due to a general decline in interest rates, the 2011 Bonds can be refinanced with lower cost debt.  This will allow the City 
to save approximately 10% in debt service. 
Plan of Finance
The Project
California cities may not pledge their general fund revenues to repay debt obligations that extend 13 months beyond the 
end of a fiscal year unless such obligations have been approved by a 2/3rd vote of the electorate. However, because 
California courts have ruled that a lease is not a debt, cities may enter into multi-year leases secured by their general 
funds.  To finance the Project, the City will enter into a 30-year lease payable from its General Fund, and apply Measure S 
sales tax revenues to make the lease payments.
Generally, the project or projects being financed in a lease financing serve as the asset being leased.  However, because a 
city cannot legally be obligated to make lease payments until it has beneficial use of the asset being leased, using the 
Project as the leased asset for this financing would require that all interest due during the construction period for the 
Project be borrowed, increasing the size of the financing and the amount of interest paid.  Additionally, an assortment of 
financed assets comprising pump stations, levee improvements for North Shoreview and South Bayfront, and street 
reconstruction projects makes for a more complicated presentation to investors than does the leasing of a single, essential 
city asset.  
Consequently, the City’s municipal advisors have recommended that the City use its main library, which is equivalent in 
value to the cost of the Project, as the leased asset.  Using a lease-leaseback structure, the City will, pursuant to a site 
lease, lease the library to the San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority (the “Authority”) for a sum sufficient to 
construct the Project, and the Authority will lease the library back to City pursuant to a facility lease over a 30-year period.  
Because the Authority does not have sufficient assets to make the site lease payment to the City, it will sell lease revenue 
bonds secured by the lease payments to be made by the City, and use the proceeds from the bond sale to make the site 
lease payment to the City, which the City will use to construct the Project.  The lease payments will mirror the debt service 
on the revenue bonds.
307 of 425

--- page 308 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
San Mateo - Foster City Public Financing Authority
The City previously used the City of San Mateo Joint Powers Public Authority for financings such as this.  However, that 
authority is a joint powers authority between the City and the City’s redevelopment agency, and the City’s bond counsel 
has determined that because the redevelopment agency has been dissolved and succeeded by the Successor Agency 
(which will dissolve when its final enforceable obligation has been satisfied), the existing joint powers authority cannot 
issue bonds that amortize beyond the longest maturity of the Successor Agency’s outstanding bonds, which is 2032. 
Because the 2020 Bond maturity extends beyond this date, the San Mateo – Foster City Public Financing Authority is being 
used in this financing. 
The table shown in Attachement 2 shows the estimated sources and uses of funds.
Refinancing
The 2011 Bonds were sold in the amount of $5,745,000, of which $3,795,000 remains outstanding.  By selling refunding 
bonds and using the proceeds to call the outstanding bonds on their first optional redemption date, annual debt service 
can be reduced by approximately 10% annually.  Net present value savings (“NPV Savings”), which is a savings metric that 
discounts all future year savings into a single present value savings number, is estimated at $278,274, or 7.33% of the par 
amount of bonds to be refinanced.  Best practices established by the Government Finance Officers Association and the 
City’s Debt Policy require an NPV Savings floor of 3% of the par amount of bonds to be refinanced.
REQUIRED APPROVALS
The resolution before the City Council tonight approves the Authority’s sale of revenue bonds provided that: (a) the 
aggregate principal amount of the 2020 Bonds may not exceed $54,000,000 (b) the net interest cost of the 2020A Bonds 
may not exceed 3.75%, (c) the net interest cost of the 2020B Bonds may not exceed 3.10%, (d) the underwriter's discount 
(exclusive of any original issue discount) may not exceed 0.35% of the principal amount of the 2020 Bonds, and (e) the net 
present value of debt service savings realized by the City as a result of the refunding of the 2011 Bonds will be equal to 3% 
or more of the outstanding principal amount of the 2011 Bonds.  The resolution further approves a site lease, a facility 
lease, two installment payment contracts, a bond purchase agreement, and a continuing disclosure certificate that 
obligates the City to make certain annual disclosures to investors, and approves the preliminary official statement 
describing the 2020 Bonds in substantially the form on file with the City Clerk, and the distribution of the final official 
statement by the underwriter.  The resolution further approves execution of an agreement with Jones Hall for bond 
counsel/disclosure counsel services and agreements with the firms of William Euphrat Municipal Finance, Inc. and Kitahata 
& Company as municipal advisors to the City in connection with the issuance of the 2020 Bonds.  Finally, the resolution 
identifies officers of the City who are authorized to execute any and all other such documents that are necessary for the 
sale of the 2020 Bonds.
Under applicable law, the City may approve the sale of the bonds only after holding a public hearing and making findings 
of significant public benefit from the Authority’s issuance of the bonds. 
RESPONSIBILITIES OF ELECTED OFFICIALS UNDER FEDERAL SECURITIES LAWS 
The preliminary official statement has been reviewed and approved for transmittal to the City Council and the Authority by 
staff and its financing team. The preliminary official statement must include all facts that would be material to an investor 
in the 2020 Bonds. Material information is information that would have a substantial likelihood of actual significance in the 
deliberations of a reasonable investor in deciding whether to buy or sell the 2020 Bonds.  Members of the City Council and 
the Authority should review the preliminary official statement and may question staff and consultants to make sure 
members feel comfortable that it includes all material facts, and in particular, those portions that disclose information 
relating to the City.  
The security for the 2020 Bonds is described in the sections of the preliminary official statement entitled “SECURITY FOR 
THE 2020 BONDS”; information about the City and its finances is set forth in “CITY FINANCIAL INFORMATION,” “THE 
LEASED PROPERTY,” and “Appendix A”; the key risk factors associated with the 2020 Bonds are described in the section of 
308 of 425

--- page 309 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
the preliminary official statement entitled “BOND OWNERS’ RISKS”; and certain limitations imposed by the California 
Constitution are described in “CONSTITUTIONAL AND STATUTORY LIMITATIONS ON TAXES AND APPROPRIATIONS.” 
Information about the assessment district established in the South Bayfront neighborhood to finance levee improvements 
is described in the section of the preliminary official statement entitled “THE CITY OF SAN MATEO SOUTH BAYFRONT 
LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES ASSESSMENT DISTRICT”.  Information about the assessment district established in 
the South Bayfront neighborhood to finance levee improvements is described in the section of the preliminary official 
statement entitled “THE CITY OF SAN MATEO SOUTH BAYFRONT LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES ASSESSMENT 
DISTRICT”.   Information about the assessment district established in the North Shoreview neighborhood to contribute to 
the improvements necessary to recertify the Coyote Point Levee are described in the section of the preliminary official 
statement entitled “THE CITY OF SAN MATEO NORTH SHOREVIEW LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES 
IMPROVEMENTS ASSESSMENT DISTRICT.” 
BUDGET IMPACT:
The financing consists of a new money portion, the 2020A Bonds, which will add a lease payment obligation to the General 
Fund that is new, and a refinancing portion, the 2020B Bonds, which will lower an existing lease obligation that the City 
incurred in connection with the 2011 Bonds.
New Money Portion
The sale of the 2020A Bonds will cause the City’s General Fund to assume annual lease payments that will be 
approximately $2.785 million annually.  These lease payments, which will extend through the end of fiscal year 2047-48, 
are not optional.  A portion of these lease payments will be paid from assessments levied in the North Shoreview 
Assessment District. Net General Fund payments are shown in the following table:
Fiscal Year
City Gross Lease Payment
(less North Shoreview Assessment Levy)
City Net Lease Payment
2021 – 2022
$2,785,000
$0.00
$2,785,000
2023 – 2042
$2,785,000
$197,200
$2,587,800
2043 – 2048
$2,785,000
$0.00
$2,785,000
The City will be under a legal obligation to make gross lease payment appropriations annually. The Measure S ¼ cent sales 
tax, forecast at $5.6 million in fiscal year 2019-20, is expected to be sufficient to provide the revenue necessary to make 
these payments in future years.  In the event of a recession, or a decline in retail sales activity caused by other factors, 
these lease payments, if not off-set by an increase in other General Fund revenues, will compete with discretionary 
General Fund expenditures, such as services and CIP expenditures, and may require a reduction in such competing 
expenditures.
Refinancing Portion
The City’s existing lease obligation in connection with the 2011 Bonds is approximately $500,000 annually, and is paid first 
from South Bayfront assessment levies, and then only to the extent these amounts are insufficient to pay annual debt 
service on the 2011 Bonds, from the City’s General Fund.  The South Bayfront assessment levies have never been 
insufficient and the City has never been required to make payments from its General Fund in connection with the 2011 
Bonds.  The 2020B Bonds are expected to have lower debt service than the 2011 Bonds and the benefits realized from the 
refinancing will accrue to the assessed properties in the South Bayshore Assessment District.  There will be no adverse 
financial impact to the City’s General Fund by virtue of the sale of the 2020B Bonds.
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
This resolution approving the sale of the 2020 Bonds by the San Mateo – Foster City Public Financing Authority is not a 
project subject to CEQA, because it is government fiscal activity which does not involve any commitment to any specific 
project which may result in a potentially significant physical impact on the environment (CEQA Guidelines Section 
15378(b)(4).)
NOTICE PROVIDED
All meeting noticing requirements were met.

CITY OF SAN MATEO
RESOLUTION NO. __ (2020)
APPROVING THE ISSUANCE AND SALE OF BONDS FOR $54,000,000 BY THE SAN MATEO-
FOSTER CITY PUBLIC FINANCING AUTHORITY TO PROVIDE FOR PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS 
OWNED AND OPERATED BY THE CITY AND THE REFINANCING OF OUTSTANDING BONDS
WHEREAS, the City of San Mateo (the “City”) wishes to finance the acquisition and construction of public 
improvements (the “Project”) of the City, including street and flood control improvements; and
WHEREAS, under the Benefit Assessment Act of 1982 (the “Benefit Assessment Act”), Resolution No. 46 
(2009) adopted by the City Council on April 20, 2009, and Resolution No. 122 (2009) adopted by the City Council 
on July 13, 2009, the City established the City of San Mateo South Bayfront Levee and Flood Control Facilities 
Assessment District (the “South Bayfront Levee and Flood Control Facilities Assessment District”) to finance 
flood control improvements along portions of the San Francisco Bay shoreline; and
WHEREAS, under the Benefit Assessment Act, Resolution No. 41 (2013) adopted by the City Council on 
April 1, 2013, and Resolution No. 95 (2013) adopted by the City Council on July 15, 2013, the City modified the 
boundaries of the South Bayfront Levee and Flood Control Facilities Assessment District and levied a modified 
assessment; and
WHEREAS, under the Benefit Assessment Act, Resolution No. 3 (2018) adopted by the City Council on 
January 2, 2018, and Resolution No. 34 (2018) adopted by the City Council on March 19, 2018, the City 
established the City of San Mateo North Shoreview Flood Control Facilities Improvement Assessment District 
(the “North Shoreview Flood Control Facilities Improvement Assessment District”) to finance flood control 
improvements in the North Shoreview neighborhood along portions of the San Francisco Bay shoreline; and
WHEREAS, the City previously caused the City of San Mateo Joint Powers Financing Authority (the 
“JPFA”) to issue its City of San Mateo Joint Powers Financing Authority Revenue Bonds (South Bayfront Flood 
Control Project), Series 2011A in the aggregate initial principal amount of $5,745,000 (the “2011 Bonds”) 
pursuant to a Trust Agreement, dated as of February 1, 2011 (the “2011 Trust Agreement”), by and between the 
JPFA and The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A., as trustee (the “2011 Trustee”), for the purpose of 
financing certain flood control projects known as the “South Bayfront Flood Control Project”; and
WHEREAS, the 2011 Bonds are payable from (i) installment payments made by the City to the JPFA (the 
“2011 Installment Payments”) under an Installment Payment Contract, dated as of February 1, 2011 (the “2011 
Installment Payment Contract”), and (ii) lease payments made by the City under a Facility Lease (South Bayfront 
Flood Control Project), dated as of February 1, 2011 (the “2011 Facility Lease”); and
WHEREAS, the 2011 Installment Payments are payable from benefit assessments that are levied and 
collected under the Benefit Assessment Act in the City of San Mateo South Bayfront Levee and Flood Control 
Facilities Assessment District; and
WHEREAS, in order to take advantage of current bond market conditions, the City wishes to refinance 
the 2011 Installment Payment Contract and the 2011 Facility Lease, and to cause the advance refunding of the 
2011 Bonds, and the outstanding 2011 Bonds are subject to redemption on any date on or after August 1, 2021, 
without premium; and
WHEREAS, for the purpose, among others, of creating a separate public entity that could provide 
assistance with the financing of public capital improvements to be owned and operated by the City, the City and 
the Estero Municipal Improvement District previously established the San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing

Authority (the “Authority”) under the Joint Exercise of Powers Act (Government Code §6500 et seq.) (the “Act”) 
and a Joint Exercise of Powers Agreement dated as of February 29, 2016 (the “Joint Powers Agreement”); and
WHEREAS, the City has proposed to lease to the Authority certain real property and improvements (the 
“Leased Property”) under a Site Lease (the “Site Lease”), in consideration of the payment by the Authority of an 
upfront rental payment (the “Site Lease Payment”) that is sufficient to provide funds to (a) finance the Project 
and (b) refinance the 2011 Installment Payment Contract and the 2011 Facility Lease, and refund the 2011 
Bonds; and
WHEREAS, in order to assist the City with its financing program, and to provide funds for the payment of

with Section 6584 of said Code (the “Bond Law”):
(a)

Assessment Revenues (as defined therein) to the City’s semiannual installment payments due thereunder (the 
“2020A Installment Payments”); and

Division 2 of Title 5 (the “Refunding Law”), and in order to provide revenues which are sufficient to enable the 
Authority to pay debt service on the 2020B Bonds, the Authority and the City will enter into an installment

Assessment Revenues (as defined therein) to the City’s semiannual installment payments due thereunder (the 
“2020B Installment Payments”); and
WHEREAS, in order to secure the payments of principal of and interest on the 2020 Bonds, the Authority 
proposes to lease the Leased Property back to the City under a Lease Agreement (the “Lease Agreement”), 
under which the City is obligated to pay semiannual lease payments (the “Lease Payments”) as rental for the 
Leased Property, and, pursuant to an Assignment Agreement (the “Assignment Agreement”), the Authority will 
assign substantially all of its rights under the Lease Agreement to The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, 
N.A., as trustee for the 2020 Bonds; and
WHEREAS, the City will receive a credit against its obligation to pay the Lease Payments attributable to 
the 2020A Bonds in the amount of any 2020A Installment Payments paid by the City under the 2020A 
Installment Payment Contract; and

WHEREAS, the City will receive a credit against its obligation to pay the Lease Payments attributable to

made public; and
WHEREAS, as a condition precedent to the issuance of the 2020 Bonds, Section 6586.5 of the California 
Government Code requires that the City approve the Authority’s issuance of the 2020A Bonds and make certain 
findings, and Section 6586.5 further requires that the City give the approval and make the findings only after 
holding a noticed public hearing; and
WHEREAS, as required by Section 6586.5, the City has caused publication of a notice of a public hearing 
once at least five days prior to the hearing in a newspaper of general circulation in the City; and

Section 1.
Recitals. The foregoing recitals are true and correct.
Section 2.
Findings. Pursuant to the Bond Law, and based on the information provided to the City 
Council by City staff and consultants, all as set forth in the proceedings and documents providing for the 
issuance and delivery of the 2020 Bonds, the City Council hereby finds and determines that the issuance of the 
2020 Bonds and the transactions related thereto will result in significant public benefits within the 
contemplation of Section 6586 of the Bond Law, namely, demonstrable savings in bond preparation, bond 
underwriting, and bond issuance costs.
The financing of the South Bayfront Flood Control Project was important to the public health, safety, 
and welfare of the residents of the South Bayfront Levee and Flood Control Facilities Assessment District, and 
the refinancing of the 2011 Installment Payment Contract and the 2011 Facility Lease, and refunding of the 2011 
Bonds is in the public interest and for the special benefit of the properties in the South Bayfront Levee and Flood 
Control Facilities Assessment District.
The financing of the North Shoreview Flood Control Project is important to the public health, safety, and 
welfare of the residents of the City of San Mateo North Shoreview Flood Control Facilities Improvement 
Assessment District, and is in the public interest and for the special benefit of the properties in the City of San 
Mateo North Shoreview Flood Control Facilities Improvement Assessment District.

Section 3.
Issuance of Bonds.  The City Council hereby approves the issuance of the 2020 Bonds by the 
Authority under the Bond Law in the maximum principal amount of $54,000,000 for the purpose of providing 
funds to finance the Project, refinance the 2011 Installment Payment Contract and the 2011 Facility Lease, and 
refund the 2011 Bonds, provided that (a) the aggregate principal amount of the 2020 Bonds may not exceed 
$54,000,000, (b) the net interest cost of the 2020A Bonds may not exceed 3.75%, (c) the net interest cost of the 
2020B Bonds may not exceed 3.35%, (d) the underwriter's discount (exclusive of any original issue discount) may 
not exceed 0.35% of the principal amount of the 2020 Bonds, and (e) the net present value of debt service 
savings realized by the City as a result of the refunding of the 2011 Bonds will be equal to 3% or more of the 
outstanding principal amount of the 2020 Bonds.
Section 4.
Approval of Related Financing Agreements.  The City Council hereby approves each of the 
following agreements required for the issuance and sale of the 2020 Bonds, in substantially the respective forms 
on file with the City Clerk together with any changes therein or additions thereto deemed advisable by the 
Mayor, Vice-Mayor, City Manager, Assistant City Manager, City Attorney, Finance Director, or an authorized 
representative thereof (each, an “Authorized Officer”), subject to approval as to form by the City Attorney; 
execution of the agreements by an Authorized Officer shall be conclusive evidence of the approval of any such 
changes or additions.  An Authorized Officer, acting alone, is hereby authorized and directed for and on behalf of 
the City to execute, and the City Clerk is hereby authorized and directed to attest, if applicable, the final form of 
each such agreement, as follows:

Site Lease, between the City as lessor and the Authority as lessee, under which the City 
leases the Leased Property to the Authority in consideration of the payment of an amount 
which will be applied by the Authority to finance the Project.

Lease Agreement, between the Authority as lessor and the City as lessee, under which the 
Authority leases the Leased Property back to the City and the City agrees to pay semiannual 
lease payments which are sufficient to provide revenues with which to pay principal of and 
interest on the 2020 Bonds when due.

2020A Installment Payment Contract between the Authority and the City, under which the 
City agrees to make semiannual installment payments equal to a portion of the principal of 
and interest on the 2020A Bonds when due.

2020B Installment Payment Contract between the Authority and the City, under which the 
City agrees to make semiannual installment payments which are sufficient to provide 
revenues with which to pay principal of and interest on the 2020B Bonds when due.

Continuing Disclosure Certificate, to be executed by the City for the purpose of providing 
continuing disclosure to owners of the 2020 Bonds.

Bond Purchase Agreement among the Authority, the City and Citigroup Global Markets Inc. 
(the “Underwriter”), in substantially the form on file with the City Clerk.
The City Council hereby authorizes the delivery and performance of each of the foregoing agreements.

APPENDIX A
Government Code Section 5852.1 Disclosure
The following information consists of estimates that have been provided in good faith by the City’s 
Municipal Advisors, based on the Underwriter’s market rates from December 19, 2019, plus a cushion of 
50 basis points (0.50%):
2020A Bonds
(A)  True Interest Cost of the 2020A Bonds: 3.29%
(B)  Finance Charge of the 2020A Bonds (Sum of all fees/charges paid to third parties): $441,130
(C)  Net Proceeds of 2020A Bonds to be Received (net of finance charges, reserves and capitalized interest, 
if any): $50,000,000
(D)  Total Payment Amount on the 2020A Bonds Through Maturity: $78,013,112
2020B Bonds
(A)  True Interest Cost of the 2020B Bonds: 3.09%
(B)  Finance Charge of the 2020B Bonds (Sum of all fees/charges paid to third parties): $35,770
(C)  Net Proceeds of the 2020B Bonds to be Received (net of finance charges, reserves and capitalized 
interest, if any): $3,419,230
(D)  Total Payment Amount on the 2020B Bonds Through Maturity: $4,071,191
The foregoing estimates constitute good faith estimates only. The principal amount of the 2020 Bonds, 
the true interest cost of the 2020 Bonds, the finance charges thereof, the amount of proceeds received 
therefrom and total payment amount with respect thereto may differ from such good faith estimates due 
to (a) the actual date of the sale of the 2020 Bonds being different than the date assumed for purposes of 
such estimates, (b) the actual principal amount of Bonds sold being different from the estimated amount 
used for purposes of such estimates, (c) the actual amortization of the 2020 Bonds being different than 
the amortization assumed for purposes of such estimates, (d) the actual market interest rates at the time 
of sale of the 2020 Bonds being different than those estimated for purposes of such estimates, (e) other 
market conditions, or (f) alterations in the City’s financing plan, or a combination of such factors. The 
actual date of sale of the 2020 Bonds and the actual principal amount of Bonds sold will be determined 
by the City based on the timing of the need for proceeds of the 2020 Bonds and other factors. The actual 
interest rates borne by the 2020 Bonds will depend on market interest rates at the time of sale thereof. 
The actual amortization of the 2020 Bonds will also depend, in part, on market interest rates at the time 
of sale thereof. Market interest rates are affected by economic and other factors beyond the control of 
the City.

In the opinion of Jones Hall, A Professional Law Corporation, San Francisco, California, Bond Counsel, subject, however to certain 
qualifications described herein, under existing law, the interest on the 2020A Bonds is excluded from gross income for federal income tax 
purposes, and such interest is not an item of tax preference for purposes of the federal alternative minimum. Interest on the 2020B Bonds is not 
intended by the Authority to be excluded from gross income for federal income tax purposes. In the further opinion of Bond Counsel, interest on 
the 2020 Bonds is exempt from California personal income taxes.  See “TAX MATTERS.”

Authority for Issuance.  The $______* San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority Revenue Bonds, Series 2020A (City of San 
Mateo Street and Flood Control Projects) (the “2020A Bonds”) and the $______* San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority Revenue 
Bonds, Series 2020B (City of San Mateo 2011A South Bayfront Refinancing)(Federally Taxable) (the “2020B Bonds”, and with the 2020A Bonds, 
the “2020 Bonds”) are being issued by the San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority (the “Authority”) under a resolution adopted by the 
Governing Board of the Authority on February 6, 2020, a resolution adopted by the City Council of the City of San Mateo (the “City”) on 
January 21, 2020, and an Indenture of Trust dated as of _____ 1, 2020 (the “Indenture”), by and between the Authority and The Bank of New 
York Mellon Trust Company, N.A. as trustee (the “Trustee”).  See “THE 2020 BONDS – Authority for Issuance.”
Purpose.  The 2020A Bonds are being issued to finance the acquisition and construction of public improvements of the City, including 
street and flood control improvements, and to pay the costs of issuing the 2020A Bonds.  The 2020B Bonds are being issued to defease and 
redeem the outstanding City of San Mateo Joint Powers Financing Authority Revenue Bonds (South Bayfront Flood Control Project), Series 
2011A (the “2011A Bonds”) and to pay the costs of issuing the 2020B Bonds. See “FINANCING PLAN.”
Security.  Under the Indenture, the 2020A Bonds are payable from and secured by a first pledge of and lien on the “2020A Revenues” (as 
defined in this Official Statement), consisting primarily of (i) lease payments (the “2020A Lease Payments”) made by the City under the Lease 
Agreement, dated as of _____ 1, 2020, by and between the Authority, as lessor, and the City, as lessee (the “Lease”) with respect to the lease 
of certain real property (the “Leased Property”) and (ii) installment payments (the “2020A Installment Payments”) made by the City under the 
Installment Payment Contract, dated as of _____ 1, 2020, by and between the Authority and the City (the “2020A Installment Payment Contract”), 
as further described in this Official Statement.  The 2020B Bonds are payable from and secured by a first pledge of and lien on the “2020B 
Revenues” (as defined in this Official Statement), consisting primarily of (i) lease payments (the “2020B Lease Payments” and together with the 
2020A Lease Payments, the “Lease Payments”) made by the City under the Lease with respect to the lease of the Leased Property and (ii) 
installment payments (the “2020B Installment Payments”) made by the City under the Installment Payment Contract, dated as of _____ 1, 2020, 
by and between the Authority and the City (the “2020B Installment Payment Contract”), all as further described in this Official Statement. The 
2020 Bonds are also secured by certain funds on deposit under the Indenture.  Neither the Authority nor the City is establishing a reserve fund 
for the 2020 Bonds. 
The City has covenanted in the Lease to take all actions required to include the Lease Payments in each of its budgets during the term of 
the Lease and to make the necessary appropriations for all Lease Payments and Additional Rental Payments. The Lease Payments are subject 
to complete or partial abatement in the event and to the extent that there is substantial interference with the City’s use and possession of the 
Leased Property or any portion thereof. See “SECURITY FOR THE 2020 BONDS.”  
In the 2020A Installment Payment Contract, the City has pledged the revenues from certain special benefit assessments levied within the 
City’s North Shoreview Levee and Flood Control Facilities Improvements Assessment District established pursuant to the Benefit Assessment 
Act of 1982 to make 2020A Installment Payments. The North Shoreview Assessment Revenues may be collected for a term of 20 years, which 
term is shorter than the term of the 2020A Bonds. The initial levy of the North Shoreview Assessment Revenues is expected to be in fiscal year 
2023-24. The annual amount of North Shoreview Assessment Revenues is expected to be approximately 6.5% of annual debt service on the 
2020A Bonds during the 20-year period it is collected. See “THE CITY OF SAN MATEO NORTH SHOREVIEW LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL 
FACILITIES IMPROVEMENTS ASSESSMENT DISTRICT.”
In the 2020B Installment Payment Contract, the City has pledged the revenues from certain special benefit assessments levied within the 
City’s South Bayfront Levee and Flood Control Facilities Assessment District established pursuant to the Benefit Assessment Act of 1982 to 
make 2020B Installment Payments. See “THE CITY OF SAN MATEO SOUTH BAYFRONT LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES 
ASSESSMENT DISTRICT.
Book-Entry Only.  The 2020 Bonds will be issued in book-entry form only and will be initially issued and registered in the name of Cede 
& Co. as nominee of The Depository Trust Company, New York, New York (“DTC”).  The 2020 Bonds are issuable as fully registered securities 
in denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple of $5,000.  Purchasers of the 2020 Bonds (the “Beneficial Owners”) will not receive physical 
certificates representing their interest in the 2020 Bonds.  See “THE 2020 BONDS” and “APPENDIX F - DTC AND THE BOOK-ENTRY ONLY 
SYSTEM.”
Payments.  Interest on the 2020 Bonds accrues from the date of delivery and is payable semiannually on May 1 and November 1 of each 
year, commencing November 1, 2020.  Payments of principal and interest on the 2020 Bonds will be paid by the Trustee to DTC for subsequent 
disbursement to DTC Participants, which will remit such payments to the Beneficial Owners of the 2020 Bonds.  See “THE 2020 BONDS - 
General Provisions.”
Redemption*.  The 2020A Bonds are subject to optional redemption and mandatory sinking fund payment redemption. The 2020B Bonds 
are not subject to optional or mandatory sinking fund redemption prior to maturity. Both the 2020A Bonds and the 2020B Bonds are subject to 
special mandatory redemption from insurance or condemnation proceeds prior to maturity.  See “THE 2020 BONDS – Redemption.” 
NONE OF THE 2020 BONDS, THE OBLIGATION OF THE AUTHORITY TO PAY PRINCIPAL THEREOF OR INTEREST THEREON, OR 
THE OBLIGATION OF THE CITY TO MAKE THE LEASE PAYMENTS, CONSTITUTES A DEBT OR A LIABILITY OF THE AUTHORITY, THE 
This Preliminary Official Statement and the information contained herein are subject to completion or amendment.  These securities may not be sold nor may offers to buy be accepted 
prior to the time the Official Statement is delivered in final form.  Under no circumstances will this Preliminary Official Statement constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy 
nor will there be any sale of these securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer solicitation or sale would be unlawful.
318 of 425

--- page 319 ---
CITY OR THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, OR ANY OF ITS POLITICAL SUBDIVISIONS, WITHIN THE MEANING OF ANY CONSTITUTIONAL 
LIMITATION ON INDEBTEDNESS, OR A PLEDGE OF THE FULL FAITH AND CREDIT OF THE CITY.  THE 2020 BONDS ARE SECURED 
SOLELY BY THE PLEDGE OF REVENUES AND CERTAIN FUNDS HELD UNDER THE INDENTURE.  THE 2020 BONDS ARE NOT 
SECURED BY A PLEDGE OF THE TAXING POWER OF THE CITY.
MATURITY SCHEDULE
(see inside cover)
Cover Page.  This cover page contains certain information for general reference only.  It is not a summary of all the provisions of the 2020 
Bonds.  Prospective investors must read the entire Official Statement to obtain information essential to making an informed investment decision.
The 2020 Bonds are offered when, as and if issued, and are subject to the approval as to their legality by Jones Hall, A Professional Law 
Corporation, San Francisco, California, Bond Counsel.  Certain legal matters will also be passed upon for the Authority and the City by Jones 
Hall, A Professional Law Corporation, as Disclosure Counsel, and for the Underwriter by Stradling Yocca Carlson & Rauth.  Certain legal matters 
will be passed upon for the City by the City Attorney.  It is anticipated that the 2020 Bonds will be delivered in book-entry form through the 
facilities of DTC on or about _____, 2020.
The date of this Official Statement is: ______________, 2020

GENERAL INFORMATION ABOUT THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT
Use of Official Statement.  This Official Statement is submitted in connection with the sale of the 2020 
Bonds referred to herein and may not be reproduced or used, in whole or in part, for any other purpose.  This 
Official Statement is not a contract between any bond owner and the Authority or the Underwriter. 
No Offering Except by This Official Statement.  No dealer, broker, salesperson, or other person has been 
authorized by the Authority or the Underwriter to give any information or to make any representations other than 
those contained in this Official Statement and, if given or made, such other information or representation must 
not be relied upon as having been authorized by the Authority or the Underwriter. 
No Unlawful Offers or Solicitations.  This Official Statement does not constitute an offer to sell or the 
solicitation of an offer to buy, nor may there be any sale of the 2020 Bonds by a person in any jurisdiction in 
which it is unlawful for such person to make such an offer, solicitation, or sale.
Information in Official Statement.  The information set forth in this Official Statement has been furnished 
by the Authority and other sources which are believed to be reliable, but it is not guaranteed as to accuracy or 
completeness. 
Estimates and Forecasts.  When used in this Official Statement and in any continuing disclosure by the 
Authority in any press release and in any oral statement made with the approval of an authorized officer of the 
City or the Authority or any other entity described or referenced herein, the words or phrases “will likely result,” 
“are expected to,” “will continue,” “is anticipated,” “estimate,” “project,” “forecast,” “expect,” “intend,” and similar 
expressions identify “forward looking statements” within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform 
Act of 1995.  Such statements are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ 
materially from those contemplated in such forward-looking statements.  Any forecast is subject to such 
uncertainties.  Inevitably, some assumptions used to develop the forecasts will not be realized and unanticipated 
events and circumstances may occur.  Therefore, there are likely to be differences between forecasts and actual 
results, and those differences may be material. The information and expressions of opinion herein are subject to 
change without notice, and neither the delivery of this Official Statement nor any sale made hereunder shall, 
under any circumstances, give rise to any implication that there has been no change in the affairs of the City, 
Authority, or any other entity described or referenced herein since the date hereof.
Involvement of Underwriter.  The following statement has been included in this Official Statement on behalf 
of the initial purchaser of the 2020 Bonds (the “Underwriter”): The Underwriter has reviewed the information in 
this Official Statement in accordance with, and as a part of, its responsibilities to investors under the Federal 
Securities Laws as applied to the facts and circumstances of this transaction, but the Underwriter does not 
guarantee the accuracy or completeness of such information.  
Stabilization of and Changes to Offering Prices.  The Underwriter may overallot or take other steps that 
stabilize or maintain the market prices of the 2020 Bonds at levels above that which might otherwise prevail in 
the open market.  If commenced, the Underwriter may discontinue such market stabilization at any time.  The 
Underwriter may offer and sell the 2020 Bonds to certain securities dealers, dealer banks, and banks acting as 
agent at prices lower than the public offering prices stated on the inside cover page of this Official Statement, 
and those public offering prices may be changed from time to time by the Underwriter.
Document Summaries.  All summaries of the Indenture or other documents referred to in this Official 
Statement are made subject to the provisions of such documents and qualified in their entirety by reference to 
such documents, and do not purport to be complete statements of any or all of such provisions.
No Securities Laws Registration.  The 2020 Bonds have not been registered under the Securities Act of 
1933, as amended, in reliance upon exceptions therein for the issuance and sale of municipal securities.  The 
2020 Bonds have not been registered or qualified under the securities laws of any state.
Effective Date.  This Official Statement speaks only as of its date, and the information and expressions of 
opinion contained in this Official Statement are subject to change without notice.  Neither the delivery of this 
Official Statement nor any sale of the 2020 Bonds will, under any circumstances, give rise to any implication that 
there has been no change in the affairs of the City, the Authority, the other parties described in this Official 
Statement, or the condition of the property within the City since the date of this Official Statement.

The purpose of this Official Statement, which includes the cover page, inside cover page, 
and attached appendices, is to set forth certain information concerning the sale and delivery of 
the $______* San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority Revenue Bonds, Series 2020A 
(City of San Mateo Street and Flood Control Projects) (the “2020A Bonds”) and the $______* 
San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority Revenue Bonds, Series 2020B (City of San 
Mateo 2011A South Bayfront Refinancing)(Federally Taxable) (the “2020B Bonds”, and with the 
2020A Bonds, the “2020 Bonds”) by the San Mateo-Foster City Public Financing Authority (the 
“Authority”).  All capitalized terms used in this Official Statement, unless noted otherwise, have 
the meanings set forth in the Indenture (as defined below).
INTRODUCTION
This introduction is not a summary of this Official Statement.  It is only a brief description 
of and guide to, and is qualified by, more complete and detailed information contained in the entire 
Official Statement and the documents summarized or described herein.  A full review should be 
made of the entire Official Statement.  The offering of the 2020 Bonds to potential investors is 
made only by means of the entire Official Statement.
Authority for Issuance. The Authority is issuing the 2020 Bonds under the following: 
(a)
Article 4 of Chapter 5 of Division 7 of Title 1 of the California Government 
Code, as amended, commencing with Section 6584 (the “Bond Law”),  
(b)
resolutions adopted by the Governing Board (the “Board”) of the Authority 
on February 6, 2020 (the “Authority Resolution”), and by the City Council (the “City 
Council”) of the City of San Mateo (the “City”) on January 21, 2020 (the “City 
Resolution”), and
(c)
an Indenture of Trust (the “Indenture”) dated as of _____ 1, 2020, by and 
between the Authority and The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A., as trustee 
(the “Trustee”). 
The Authority.  The Authority is a joint exercise of powers authority between the City and 
the Estero Municipal Improvement District (the “District”, and collectively with the City, the 
“Members”) pursuant to a Joint Exercise of Powers Agreement dated as of February 29, 2016, 
326 of 425

--- page 327 ---
2
entered into under Articles 1 and 4 (commencing with Section 6500) of Chapter 5 of Division 7 of 
Title 1 of the California Government Code, as amended, for the purpose, among others, of 
financing and refinancing the cost of public capital improvements owned and operated by its 
Members. 
The City.  The City is a charter city that was incorporated in 1894.  It comprises 
approximately 14.6 square miles located on the San Francisco Peninsula in San Mateo County 
(the “County”), approximately 20 miles south of the City of San Francisco and 30 miles north of 
the City of San José, and abuts the northern end of Silicon Valley.  For demographic and financial 
information regarding the City and County, see “APPENDIX A – GENERAL INFORMATION 
ABOUT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN MATEO,” and “APPENDIX C – FISCAL YEAR 2018-
19 COMPREHENSIVE ANNUAL FINANCIAL REPORT.” 
Purpose of the 2020 Bonds. 
•
The 2020A Bonds are being issued to finance the acquisition and construction 
of public improvements of the City, including street and flood control 
improvements, and to pay the costs of issuing the 2020A Bonds; and 
•
The 2020B Bonds are being issued to defease and redeem the City of San 
Mateo Joint Powers Financing Authority Revenue Bonds (South Bayfront 
Flood Control Project), Series 2011A (the “2011A Bonds”), which are currently 
outstanding in the principal amount of $_____, and to pay the costs of issuing 
the 2020B Bonds.
See “FINANCING PLAN.”
Security for the 2020 Bonds and Pledge of Revenues.  Under the Indenture, the 2020A 
Bonds are payable from and secured by a first pledge of and lien on the 2020A Revenues (as 
defined in this Official Statement), consisting primarily of (i) lease payments (the “2020A Lease 
Payments”) made by the City under the Lease Agreement, dated as of _____ 1, 2020, by and 
between the Authority, as lessor, and the City, as lessee (the “Lease”) with respect to the Leased 
Property and (ii) installment payments (the “2020A Installment Payments”) made by the City 
under the Installment Payment Contract, dated as of _____ 1, 2020, by and between the Authority 
and the City (the “2020A Installment Payment Contract”), as described below. 
The City’s obligation to make a 2020A Lease Payment on any Lease Payment Date shall 
be deemed satisfied to the extent of the scheduled 2020A Installment Payments received by the 
Trustee as of such Lease Payment Date. See “SECURITY FOR THE 2020 BONDS” and “THE 
CITY OF SAN MATEO NORTH SHOREVIEW LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES 
IMPROVEMENTS ASSESSMENT DISTRICT”.  
The 2020B Bonds are payable from and secured by a first pledge of and lien on the 2020B 
Revenues (as defined in this Official Statement), consisting primarily of (i) lease payments (the 
“2020B Lease Payments”) made by the City under the Lease with respect to the lease of the 
Leased Property and (ii) installment payments (the “2020B Installment Payments”) made by the 
City under the Installment Payment Contract, dated as of _____ 1, 2020, by and between the 
Authority and the City (the “2020B Installment Payment Contract”). 
327 of 425

--- page 328 ---
3
Upon the refinancing of the 2011A Bonds with the proceeds of the 2020B Bonds, the 
South Bayfront Assessment Revenues will cease to pay the installment payments securing the 
2011A Bonds and will thereafter pay the 2020B Installment Payments securing the 2020B Bonds. 
The City’s obligation to make a 2020B Lease Payment on any Lease Payment Date shall 
be deemed satisfied to the extent of the scheduled 2020B Installment Payments received by the 
Trustee as of such Lease Payment Date. See “SECURITY FOR THE 2020 BONDS” and “THE 
CITY OF SAN MATEO SOUTH BAYFRONT LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES 
ASSESSMENT DISTRICT”.  
The 2020 Bonds are also secured by certain funds on deposit under the Indenture.  Neither 
the Authority nor the City is establishing a reserve fund for the 2020 Bonds. See “SECURITY FOR 
THE 2020 BONDS.”
The City and the Authority will enter into a Site Lease dated as of _____ 1, 2020 (the “Site 
Lease”).  Under the Site Lease, the City will lease certain real property to the Authority, consisting 
of the San Mateo Main Public Library (as described herein, the “Leased Property”).  
Concurrently, the City and the Authority will enter into the Lease, under which the Authority will 
lease the Leased Property back to the City for the purpose of financing the 2020 Bonds.  See 
“THE LEASED PROPERTY.”
Lease Covenant to Appropriate.  The City has covenanted in the Lease to take all 
actions required to include the Lease Payments in each of its budgets during the Term of the 
Lease and to make the necessary appropriations for all Lease Payments and Additional Rental 
Payments.  See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS.”
2020A Installment Payments.  Pursuant to the 2020A Installment Payment Contract, the 
City has agreed to pay the Authority the 2020A Installment Payments for the acquisition and 
construction of a portion of the North Shoreview Flood Control project.  The 2020A Installment 
Payments are secured by a first pledge and lien upon the North Shoreview Assessment 
Revenues, which consist of all revenue received by the City from the levy and collection of the 
North Shoreview Benefit Assessments.  The North Shoreview Benefit Assessments are annual 
assessments levied within the City of San Mateo North Shoreview Levee and Flood Control 
Facilities Improvements Assessment District (“North Shoreview Assessment District”) 
pursuant to the Benefit Assessment Act of 1982 (the “Benefit Assessment Act”).  See “THE 
CITY OF SAN MATEO NORTH SHOREVIEW LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES 
IMPROVEMENTS ASSESSMENT DISTRICT.”  
The City has covenanted in the 2020A Installment Payment Contract to annually levy, and 
present in proper format to the County Auditor of the County, the annual assessments authorized 
to be levied under the authority of the North Shoreview Assessment District on a timely basis so 
that the assessment amounts may be properly placed on the County’s annual secured property 
tax bills for the properties within the North Shoreview Assessment District.  Subject to the 
maximum authorized rates, the City will levy the assessments in an amount sufficient to pay the 
scheduled 2020A Installment Payments when due. 
The North Shoreview Assessment Revenues may be collected for a term of 20 years, 
which term is shorter than the term of the 2020A Bonds. The initial levy of the North Shoreview 
Assessment Revenues is expected to be in fiscal year 2023-24. The annual amount of North 
Shoreview Assessment Revenues is expected to be approximately 6.5% of annual debt service 
on the 2020A Bonds during the 20-year period in which such revenues are collected.
328 of 425

--- page 329 ---
4
2020B Installment Payments.  Pursuant to the 2020B Installment Payment Contract, the 
City has agreed to pay the Authority the 2020B Installment Payments to refinance the acquisition, 
design, construction, and installation of the South Bayfront Flood Control Project, as described 
below in “FINANCING PLAN”.  The 2020B Installment Payments are secured by a first pledge 
and lien upon the South Bayfront Assessment Revenues, which consist of all revenue received 
by the City from the levy and collection of the South Bayfront Benefit Assessments.  The South 
Bayfront Benefit Assessments are annual assessments levied within the City of San Mateo South 
Bayfront Levee and Flood Control Facilities Assessment District (the “South Bayfront 
Assessment District”) pursuant to the Benefit Assessment Act.  See “THE CITY OF SAN 
MATEO SOUTH BAYFRONT LEVEE AND FLOOD CONTROL FACILITIES ASSESSMENT 
DISTRICT.” 
The City has covenanted in the 2020B Installment Payment Contract to annually levy, and 
present in proper format to the County Auditor of the County, the annual assessments authorized 
to be levied under the authority of the South Bayfront Assessment District on a timely basis so 
that the assessment amounts may be properly placed on the County’s annual secured property 
tax bills for the properties within the South Bayfront Assessment District.  Subject to the maximum 
authorized rates, the City will levy the assessments in an amount sufficient to pay the scheduled 
2020B Installment Payments when due. 
Form of Bonds; Book-Entry Only.  The 2020 Bonds will be issued in fully registered 
form, registered in the name of The Depository Trust Company, New York, New York (“DTC”), or 
its nominee, which will act as securities depository for the 2020 Bonds.  Purchasers of the 2020 
Bonds will not receive certificates representing the 2020 Bonds that are purchased.  See “THE 
2020 BONDS - Book-Entry Only System” and “APPENDIX F – DTC AND THE BOOK-ENTRY 
ONLY SYSTEM.”
Redemption*.  The 2020A Bonds are subject to optional redemption and mandatory 
redemption from sinking fund payments. The 2020B Bonds are not subject to optional redemption 
or mandatory redemption from sinking fund payments prior to maturity. Both the 2020A Bonds 
and the 2020B Bonds are subject to special mandatory redemption from the proceeds of 
insurance or condemnation proceeds prior to their stated maturity dates.  See “THE 2020 BONDS 
– Redemption.”
Abatement.  The Lease Payments are subject to complete or partial abatement in the 
event and to the extent that there is substantial interference with the City’s use and possession 
of the Leased Property or any portion thereof.  If the Lease Payments are abated under the Lease, 
the Bond Owners would receive less than the full amount of principal of and interest on the 2020 
Bonds.  To the extent proceeds of rental interruption insurance are available (as described below), 
Lease Payments (or a portion thereof) may be made from those proceeds during periods of 
abatement.  See “SECURITY FOR THE 2020 BONDS – Abatement” and “BOND OWNERS’ 
RISKS.”
Legal Opinion.  Upon delivery of the 2020 Bonds, Jones Hall, A Professional Law 
Corporation, San Francisco, California, Bond Counsel (“Bond Counsel”) will release its final 
approving legal opinion with respect to the 2020 Bonds, regarding the validity of the 2020 Bonds 
and tax-exempt status of the 2020A Bonds, in the form attached hereto as APPENDIX D. 
* Preliminary; subject to change.
329 of 425

--- page 330 ---
5
Risks of Investment.  Debt service on the 2020A Bonds is payable only from the 2020A 
Lease Payments, the 2020A Installment Payments, and other amounts payable by the City to the 
Authority under the Lease.  Debt service on the 2020B Bonds is payable only from the 2020B 
Lease Payments, the 2020B Installment Payments, and other amounts payable by the City to the 
Authority under the Lease.  For a discussion of some of the risks associated with the purchase of 
the 2020 Bonds, see “BOND OWNERS’ RISKS.” 
NONE OF THE 2020 BONDS, THE OBLIGATION OF THE AUTHORITY TO PAY 
PRINCIPAL THEREOF OR INTEREST THEREON, OR THE OBLIGATION OF THE CITY TO 
MAKE THE LEASE PAYMENTS CONSTITUTES A DEBT OR A LIABILITY OF THE 
AUTHORITY, THE CITY, OR THE STATE, OR ANY OF ITS POLITICAL SUBDIVISIONS, 
WITHIN THE MEANING OF ANY CONSTITUTIONAL LIMITATION ON INDEBTEDNESS, OR A 
PLEDGE OF THE FULL FAITH AND CREDIT OF THE CITY.  THE 2020 BONDS ARE SECURED 
SOLELY BY THE PLEDGE OF REVENUES AND CERTAIN FUNDS HELD UNDER THE 
INDENTURE.  THE 2020 BONDS ARE NOT SECURED BY A PLEDGE OF THE TAXING 
POWER OF THE CITY. 
THE CITY’S OBLIGATION TO MAKE 2020A LEASE PAYMENTS AND 2020B LEASE 
PAYMENTS, WHICH HAVE BEEN SCHEDULED TO SATISFY DEBT SERVICE ON THE 2020A 
BONDS AND THE 2020B BONDS, RESPECTIVELY, IS SUBJECT TO THE CITY’S BENEFICIAL 
USE OF THE LEASED PROPERTY. NORTH SHOREVIEW BENEFIT ASSESSMENT LEVIES 
AND SOUTH BAYFRONT BENEFIT ASSESSMENT LEVIES RECEIVED BY THE CITY IN 
CONNECTION WITH THE 2020A INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS AND 2020B INSTALLMENT 
PAYMENTS, RESPECTIVELY, WILL BE USED TO OFFSET THE CITY’S LEASE PAYMENT 
OBLIGATIONS. HOWEVER, THERE CAN BE NO ASSURANCE THAT THE NORTH 
SHOREVIEW BENEFIT ASSESSMENT LEVIES AND THE SOUTH BAYFRONT BENEFIT 
ASSESSMENT LEVIES WILL BE PAID AS SCHEDULED. THE CITY’S OBLIGATION TO PAY 
THE 2020A INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS AND THE 2020B INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS IS A 
SPECIAL OBLIGATION OF THE CITY, LIMITED SOLELY TO THE SOUTH BAYFRONT 
ASSESSMENT REVENUES AND THE NORTH SHOREVIEW ASSESSMENT REVENUES, 
RESPECTIVELY.  THE CITY’S GENERAL FUND WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR LEASE 
PAYMENTS MADE TO MAKE UP FOR INSTALLMENT PAYMENT DEFICIENCIES.

FINANCING PLAN
Purpose of 2020 Bonds
The 2020A Bonds are being issued to finance the acquisition and construction of public 
improvements of the City, including street and flood control improvements, and to pay the costs 
of issuing the 2020A Bonds. 
The 2020B Bonds are being issued to defease and redeem the 2011A Bonds, and to pay 
the costs of issuing the 2020B Bonds. 
On the Closing Date, a portion of the proceeds of the 2020B Bonds will be deposited into 
an escrow fund (the “Escrow Fund”) established and held under an escrow agreement (the 
“Escrow Agreement”) among the Authority, the City, and the Bank of New York Mellon Trust 
Company, N.A., as escrow agent (the “Escrow Agent”), which deposit, together with interest 
earned thereon, will be sufficient to pay debt service on the 2011A Bonds prior to their first optional 
redemption date. Pursuant to the Escrow Agreement, the amount deposited into the Escrow Fund 
will be held as cash or invested by the Escrow Agent in federal securities. The maturing principal 
of and interest on such federal securities will be sufficient, together with amounts held as cash in 
the Escrow Fund, to redeem the 2011A Bonds on August 1, 2021 (the “Redemption Date”).
Sufficiency of the deposit in the Escrow Fund to prepay and redeem the 2011A Bonds on 
the Redemption Date will be verified by Causey Demgen & Moore P.C., Denver, Colorado, (the 
“Verification Agent”). See “VERIFICATION OF MATHEMATICAL ACCURACY” below.

The estimated sources and uses of funds relating to the 2020 Bonds are as follows:

THE LEASED PROPERTY
Description and Location 
Lease Payments will be made by the City under the Lease for the use and occupancy of 
the San Mateo Main Public Library (the “Leased Property”).  
Description of the Leased Property.  The Leased Property consists of the San Mateo 
Main Public Library, located at 55 West 3rd Avenue, San Mateo, CA 94402, in the County of San 
Mateo. The Leased Property has an insured value of $57,154,967. 
Public Library Service Encumbrance. The City has covenanted in the Lease to comply 
with the requirements of the Public Library Service Encumbrance so long as the City of San Mateo 
Main Public Library remains subject thereto and is an element of the Leased Property. “Public 
Library Service Encumbrance” means that certain Deed Restriction on Real Property Upon 
Which is Located the City of San Mateo Main Public Library, dated March 13, 2007, and recorded 
against the San Mateo Main Public Library property on March 16, 2007, as document 2007-
040227 in the Official Records of San Mateo County.
Modification of Leased Property
Under the Lease, the City has the right, at its own expense, to make additions, 
modifications, and improvements to the Leased Property or any portion thereof.  All additions, 
modifications, and improvements to the Leased Property will thereafter comprise part of the 
Leased Property and become subject to the provisions of the Lease.  
Such additions, modifications, and improvements may not in any way damage the Leased 
Property, interfere with the City’s beneficial use and occupancy of the Leased Property, or cause 
the Leased Property to be used for purposes other than those authorized under the provisions of 
state and federal law; and the Leased Property, upon completion of any additions, modifications 
and improvements made thereto under this provision of the Lease, must be of a value which is 
not substantially less than the value thereof immediately prior to the making of such additions, 
modifications, and improvements.  
The City will not permit any mechanic’s or other lien to be established or remain against 
the Leased Property for labor or materials furnished in connection with any remodeling, additions, 
modifications, improvements, repairs, renewals, or replacements made by the City under this 
provision of the Lease; except that if any such lien is established and the City first notifies or 
causes to be notified the Authority of the City’s intention to do so, the City may in good faith 
contest any lien filed or established against the Leased Property, and in such event may permit 
the items so contested to remain undischarged and unsatisfied during the period of such contest 
and any appeal therefrom and will provide the Authority with full security against any loss or 
forfeiture which might arise from the nonpayment of any such item, in form satisfactory to the 
Authority.  The Authority will cooperate fully in any such contest, upon the request and at the 
expense of the City.
Substitution
Under the Lease, the City has the option at any time, and from time to time, to substitute 
other real property (the “Substitute Property”) for the Leased Property or any portion thereof (the 
332 of 425

--- page 333 ---
8
“Former Property”), upon satisfaction of all of the requirements set forth in the Lease, which 
include (among others) the following:
•
The City has filed with the Authority and the Trustee, and caused to be recorded 
in the office of the County Recorder, sufficient memorialization of an amendment 
of the Lease, the Site Lease, and the Assignment Agreement that adds the legal 
description of the Substitute Property and deletes therefrom the legal description 
of the Former Property, and has filed and caused to be recorded corresponding 
amendments to the Site Lease and Assignment Agreement.
•
The City has obtained a CLTA policy of title insurance insuring the City’s leasehold 
estate under the Lease in the Substitute Property, subject only to Permitted 
Encumbrances (as defined in the Lease), in an amount at least equal to the 
estimated value thereof.
•
The City has certified in writing to the Authority and the Trustee that the Substitute 
Property serves the municipal purposes of the City and constitutes property which 
the City is permitted to lease under the laws of the State of California, and has 
been determined to be essential to the proper, efficient, and economic operation 
of the City and to serve an essential governmental function of the City.
•
The City has filed with the Authority and the Trustee a written certificate of the City 
or other written evidence stating that the useful life of the Substitute Property at 
least extends to the final maturity date of the 2020 Bonds, that the estimated value 
of the Leased Property, after substitution of the Substitute Property and release of 
the Former Property, is at least equal to the aggregate Outstanding principal 
amount of the 2020 Bonds, and the fair rental value of the Leased Property, after 
substitution of the Substitute Property and release of the Former Property, is at 
least equal to the Lease Payments thereafter coming due and payable under the 
Lease.
See “APPENDIX B – SUMMARY OF PRINCIPAL LEGAL DOCUMENTS.”
Upon the satisfaction of all such conditions precedent, the Term of the Lease will 
thereupon end as to the Former Property and commence as to the Substitute Property, and all 
references to the Former Property will apply with full force and effect to the Substitute Property.  
The City is not entitled to any reduction, diminution, extension, or other modification of the Lease 
Payments whatsoever as a result of any substitution of property under this provision of the Lease.  
Release of Leased Property
Under the Lease, the City has the option at any time, and from time to time to release any 
portion of the Leased Property from the Lease (the “Released Property”) provided that the City 
has satisfied all of the requirements under the Lease that are conditions precedent to such 
release, which include (among others) the following:
•
The City has filed with the Authority and the Trustee, and caused to be recorded 
in the office of the County Recorder sufficient memorialization of an amendment 
of the Lease, the Site Lease, and the Assignment Agreement which removes the 
Released Property from the Lease, the Site Lease, and the Assignment 
Agreement.
333 of 425

--- page 334 ---
9
•
The City has certified in writing to the Authority and the Trustee that the value of 
the property which remains subject to the Lease following such release is at least 
equal to the aggregate Outstanding principal amount of the 2020 Bonds, and the 
fair rental value of the property which remains subject to the Lease following such 
release is at least equal to the Lease Payments thereafter coming due and payable 
thereunder.
See “APPENDIX B – SUMMARY OF PRINCIPAL LEGAL DOCUMENTS.”
Addition of Property
Under the Lease, the City may, at any time it deems it necessary or advisable, amend the 
Lease, and enter into any necessary or advisable site or ground lease, to add additional property 
to the property originally leased thereunder. 
See “APPENDIX B – SUMMARY OF PRINCIPAL LEGAL DOCUMENTS.”

THE 2020 BONDS
This section provides summaries of the 2020 Bonds and certain provisions of the 
Indenture.  See APPENDIX B for a more complete summary of the Indenture.  Capitalized terms 
used but not defined in this section have the meanings given in APPENDIX B.  
Authority for Issuance
The 2020 Bonds are being issued under the Bond Law, the Authority Resolution (which 
was adopted by the Board of the Authority on February 6, 2020), the City Resolution (which was 
adopted by the City Council on January 21, 2020), and the Indenture.  Under the Authority 
Resolution and the City Resolution, the 2020 Bonds may be issued in a principal amount not to 
exceed $54,000,000. 
General Provisions
Bond Terms.  The 2020 Bonds will be dated their date of delivery and issued in fully 
registered form without coupons in integral multiples of $5,000, so long as no 2020 Bond has 
more than one maturity date.  
Interest on the 2020 Bonds will be payable on May 1 and November 1 in each year, 
commencing November 1, 2020 (each an “Interest Payment Date”).  The 2020 Bonds will mature 
in the amounts and on the dates, and bear interest at the annual rates, set forth on the inside 
cover page of this Official Statement.
Calculation of Interest.  Interest on the 2020 Bonds is payable from the Interest Payment 
Date next preceding the date of authentication thereof unless:
(a)
a 2020 Bond is authenticated on or before an Interest Payment Date and 
after the close of business on the preceding Record Date, in which event it will bear 
interest from such Interest Payment Date,
(b)
a 2020 Bond is authenticated on or before the first Record Date, in which 
event interest thereon will be payable from the Closing Date, or
(c)
interest on any 2020 Bond is in default as of the date of authentication 
thereof, in which event interest thereon will be payable from the date to which interest has 
been paid in full, payable on each Interest Payment Date.
Interest with respect to the 2020 Bonds will be computed on the basis of a 360-day year 
composed of 12 months of 30 days each.
Record Date.  Under the Indenture, “Record Date” means, with respect to any Interest 
Payment Date, the 15th calendar day of the month preceding such Interest Payment Date, 
whether or not such day is a Business Day.
Payments of Principal and Interest.  Interest is payable on each Interest Payment Date 
to the persons in whose names the ownership of the 2020 Bonds is registered on the Registration 
Books at the close of business on the immediately preceding Record Date, except as provided 
below.  Interest on any 2020 Bond which is not punctually paid or duly provided for on any Interest 
Payment Date is payable to the person in whose name the ownership of such 2020 Bond is 
335 of 425

--- page 336 ---
11
registered on the Registration Books at the close of business on a special record date for the 
payment of such defaulted interest to be fixed by the Trustee, notice of which is given to such 
Owner by first-class mail not less than 10 days prior to such special record date.
The Trustee will pay interest on the 2020 Bonds by check of the Trustee mailed by first 
class mail, postage prepaid, on each Interest Payment Date to the Owners of the 2020 Bonds at 
their respective addresses shown on the Registration Books as of the close of business on the 
preceding Record Date.  At the written request of the Owner of 2020 Bonds in an aggregate 
principal amount of at least $1,000,000, which written request is on file with the Trustee as of any 
Record Date, the Trustee will pay interest on such 2020 Bonds on each succeeding Interest 
Payment Date by wire transfer in immediately available funds to such account of a financial 
institution within the United States of America as specified in such written request, which will 
remain in effect until rescinded in writing by the Owner.  
The Trustee will pay principal of the 2020 Bonds in lawful money of the United States of 
America by check of the Trustee upon presentation and surrender thereof at the Office of the 
Trustee.
While the 2020 Bonds are subject to the book-entry system, the principal, interest, and 
any redemption premium with respect to the 2020 Bonds will be paid by the Trustee to DTC for 
subsequent disbursement to beneficial owners of the 2020 Bonds.  See “– Book-Entry Only 
System” below.
Redemption*
Optional Redemption.  
The 2020A Bonds maturing on or before May 1, 20__, are not subject to optional 
redemption prior to their stated maturity.  The 2020A Bonds maturing on or after May 1, 20__, are 
subject to redemption, as a whole or in part at the election of the Authority among maturities on 
such basis as designated by the Authority and by lot within a maturity, at the option of the 
Authority, on May 1, 20__, and on any date thereafter, at a redemption price equal to 100% of the 
principal amount of 2020A Bonds to be redeemed, together with accrued interest thereon to the 
date fixed for redemption, without premium.
The 2020B Bonds are not subject to optional redemption prior to their stated maturity 
dates.
Special Mandatory Redemption from Insurance or Condemnation Proceeds.  The 
2020 Bonds are subject to redemption as a whole, or in part on a pro rata basis among series 
and maturities, on any date, from any Net Proceeds required to be used for such purpose as 
provided in the Indenture, at a redemption price equal to 100% of the principal amount thereof 
plus interest accrued thereon to the date fixed for redemption, without premium.
Mandatory Sinking Fund Redemption.  
The 2020A Term Bonds are subject to mandatory redemption in part by lot, at a 
redemption price equal to 100% of the principal amount thereof to be redeemed, without premium, 
* Preliminary; subject to change.

in the aggregate respective principal amounts and on May 1 in the respective years as set forth 
in the following tables; provided, however, that if some but not all of the 2020A Term Bonds have 
been redeemed through an optional redemption or special mandatory redemption from insurance 
or condemnation proceeds, the total amount of all future sinking fund payments will be reduced 
by the aggregate principal amount of the 2020A  Term Bonds so redeemed, to be allocated among 
such sinking fund payments on a pro rata basis in integral multiples of $5,000 (as set forth in a 
schedule provided by the Authority to the Trustee).
